_comment: InitechOS chrome metrics -- LOCKED spec-data (CLAUDE.md Rule 8).
Ref: PRD Sec 6.3 (Toolbox chrome), PRD Sec 10 (chrome metrics measured
from stills), PRD Appendix A (Mac pinstripe title bars -> chrome
metrics, M4).
schema_version 2 = v0->v1 uplift (beads initech-k8o5.9, 2026-06-19).
DELIBERATE ACT: this is a Rule 8 locked-spec change tracked by beads
initech-k8o5.9 (chrome_metrics v1) + initech-fb47 (chimera element map).
Structure:
frame_pixels  -- v0 measurements from spec/assets/preview.webp (1456x819).
CAVEAT: the frame is a CRT photo, NOT a linear upscale
of a 640x480 buffer. The OS window in the photo is not
full-screen, so back-computing native px from frame px
yields wrong numbers (24/19 title-bar gap proves this --
implied scale 1.26x, not the naive 1456/640=2.28x).
Keep for traceability only; do NOT use as oracle datums.
Ref: docs/research/gui-ground-truth.md Sec 3.4.
native        -- System 7.0/7.1 color chrome constants, source-cited.
These drive test-chrome (ADR-0004 D-8, FO-2/AM-3).
Every value is a per-value object {value, unit, source, note}.
golden_resolves -- metrics that CANNOT be sourced from WDEF/IM and need
a real emulator screendump (beads pvo4 / initech-q0gy).
Rule 12: ASCII-clean. No smart quotes, no non-ASCII characters.
Ref: StandardWDEF.a FIRST-HAND FETCHED 2026-06-19 from
https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/samplecode/System_7.0_WDEF/Listings/StandardWDEF_a.html
Ref: GetMBarHeight FIRST-HAND FETCHED 2026-06-19 from
https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/mac/Toolbox/Toolbox-128.html
Inside Macintosh: Macintosh Toolbox Essentials PDF fetch returned binary only
(PDF not text-extractable by WebFetch); Toolbox-303/313 values below are
brief-sourced (docs/research/gui-ground-truth.md Sec 3.3) pending a
local PDF text extraction. WDEF constants are first-hand verified.
schema_version 3 = P1-4 uplift (beads initech-dh5k.4, 2026-06-21).
DELIBERATE ACT (Rule 8): resolved chrome RGBs moved OUT of golden_resolves
into native.* locked values; each era-tagged + provenance-noted.
Sources: ../system7-decomp/specs/chrome/pinstripe.md (era-stable 7.0.1
via mint-005; goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png + mint-results-005.md),
../system7-decomp/specs/chrome/close-zoom-box.md,
../system7-decomp/specs/chrome/grow-box.md,
../system7-decomp/specs/chrome/scrollbar.md,
../win31-decomp/specs/chrome/system-metrics.md (verified DOSBox-X goldens).
Provenance note: values cited from the ../system7-decomp and ../win31-decomp
goldens corpora; pixel values resolved from those corpora goldens (not from
a first-hand fetch of refs/ into this repo -- refs/ was not persisted here).
target_system: Apple System 7.0/7.1 color chrome (WDEF minColorDepth=8 satisfied by ADR-0004 OD-2 indexed-8 640x480)

_caveat: FRAME pixels, NOT native OS pixels. The frame is a CRT photograph;
the OS window is not a full-screen 640x480 buffer in the still.
Back-computing native px from these values produces wrong numbers.
Retained for traceability only. Ref: gui-ground-truth.md Sec 3.4.

menubar_height_px.measured_from: col x=905 vertical scan: black border ends y72, menu-bar fill y73..y91, dark DESKTOP-window top frame line y92..y94. Fill band = 73..91 -> 19 px.
menubar_height_px.role: height of the top Photoshop-style menu bar

titlebar_height_px.measured_from: FILE COPY dialog, col x=620 vertical scan: top frame line y305..y306, pinstripe band y307..y329, dark bottom frame line y328..y329, body white from y331. Title bar (frame-to-frame) = 306..329 -> ~24 px. The DESKTOP window agreed at ~26 px (col x=420, y93..y119); 24 taken as the standard-dialog value.
titlebar_height_px.role: height of an active window/dialog title bar (pinstripe band incl. frame lines)

window_border_px.measured_from: FILE COPY left edge, row y=380 horizontal scan: dark frame line ~x501, light groove x503..x504, inner dark line x506..x507, body white from x510. The window frame is a ~2 px dark line plus a 1 px groove (classic Mac double-line frame).
window_border_px.role: thickness of the window outline / frame line

pinstripe_thickness_px.measured_from: Title-bar pinstripe is alternating 1 px light/dark scanlines: e.g. FILE COPY col x=620 shows the ~1 px luminance ripple across y307..y327 (period ~2 px). v0 records the stripe thickness; full period is an m4-7 metric.
pinstripe_thickness_px.role: thickness of a single title-bar pinstripe scanline

_provenance: System 7.0/7.1 color chrome at indexed-8 640x480 (ADR-0004 OD-2/OD-3).
Primary source: StandardWDEF.a (Apple System 7.0 WDEF assembly).
Cited; values match documented System-7 constants; pixel values resolved
from the ../system7-decomp goldens corpus.
WDEF geometry constants (schema_version 2, 2026-06-19):
WDEF listing: cited --
https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/samplecode/System_7.0_WDEF/Listings/StandardWDEF_a.html
GetMBarHeight: cited --
https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/mac/Toolbox/Toolbox-128.html
Inside Macintosh Toolbox Essentials PDF: FETCH RETURNED BINARY ONLY --
https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/mac/pdf/MacintoshToolboxEssentials.pdf
Toolbox-303 / Toolbox-313 references are brief-sourced from
docs/research/gui-ground-truth.md Sec 3.3 pending local PDF extraction.
Inside Macintosh Imaging With QuickDraw PDF: NOT FETCHED (not needed
for chrome geometry; governs region API semantics per ADR-0005).
Chrome RGB pixel values (schema_version 3, 2026-06-21):
Cited; values match documented System-7 constants; pixel values resolved
from the ../system7-decomp goldens corpus (goldens/captures/s7_*.png,
re/mint-results-002.md through mint-005.md) and confirmed era-stable
at System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot, re/mint-results-005.md).
refs/ was NOT persisted to this repo -- provenance is the corpus files,
not a local first-hand fetch.

menubar_height.note: GetMBarHeight returns 20 px for Roman script. v0 frame_pixels value was 19 (CRT photo measurement -- superseded by this native constant per gui-ground-truth.md Sec 3.4 resolution).

titlebar_height_std.note: Standard documentProc title bar height (without small icon). v0 frame_pixels value was 24 -- the 24/19 ratio (1.26x) proves the photo is not a linear upscale of the 640x480 buffer; do NOT back-compute native values from frame pixels (gui-ground-truth.md Sec 3.4).

titlebar_height_with_icon.note: Title bar height when a small (SICN) icon is present. IconSize EQU 20 is the icon width (also FIRST-HAND VERIFIED from same source).

scrollbar_width.toolbox_fetch_status: BRIEF-SOURCED (PDF binary; pending local text extraction)
scrollbar_width.note: The scrollbar control rect is 16 px wide. The right 1 px overlaps the 1 px window frame, so the control presents 15 px of ADDED width past the content area. Use 16 px for the rendered scrollbar control rect. The 15-vs-16 discrepancy is one pixel of frame overlap, not a contradiction (gui-ground-truth.md Sec 3.4).

window_frame.fetch_status: FIRST-HAND VERIFIED 2026-06-19 (FrameRect inset + frame drawing code)
window_frame.toolbox_fetch_status: BRIEF-SOURCED (PDF binary; pending local text extraction)
window_frame.note: Classic Mac double-line frame: 1 px outer line + 1 px inner groove. The v0 frame_pixels value was 2 -- this is actually consistent: the photo captured the combined visual width of both lines.

grow_box_size.fetch_status: FIRST-HAND VERIFIED 2026-06-19 (grow box computed from scrollBarSize)
grow_box_size.toolbox_fetch_status: BRIEF-SOURCED (PDF binary; pending local text extraction)
grow_box_size.note: 16x16 px square in the bottom-right corner. The WDEF positions it using scrollBarSize-2 (14 px) inset from the content rect edges, creating a 2 px overlap with each scrollbar. The rendered fill pattern (2D hash) is golden-resolves -- needs screendump.

close_zoom_box_frame_delta.rendered_source: ../system7-decomp/specs/chrome/close-zoom-box.md Geometry table (golden s7_doc_window.png close box x=361..371, y=168..178 = 11x11 whole gadget incl bevel; x=360/372 and y=167/179 are pinstripe #F3F3F3, NOT box)
close_zoom_box_frame_delta.wdef_calculation: (titleHeight - 13) / 2 stored in WInfo.wBoxDelta; the WDEF DERIVES a 13 px box height (the vertical-gap derivation) but the gadget RENDERS 11 px tall in the golden (the InsetRect/EraseRect pair @ WDEF 1719-1728 pulls the CopyBits dest in; the lavender bevel sits INSIDE the dark frame, not outside)
close_zoom_box_frame_delta.note: value=13 is the WDEF vertical-gap derivation (wBoxDelta = (titleHeight-13)/2 centers the box). rendered_px=11 is the visible gadget size in the golden (close-zoom-box.md, LAW 2: the golden wins over the WDEF derivation). Horizontal: close box left = struct.left + 9 (PlotGoAway 'moveq #9,D1' WDEF @1675-1678); zoom box left = struct.right - 20 (PlotZoom 'left := right; moveq #-20,D1' WDEF @1682-1693). Box top = struct.top + wBoxDelta + 1 (WDEF @1705-1707). The gadget is a double-beveled 3-D square: dark outer top/left frame + inner lavender bevel highlight + inner-right/inner-bottom dark + lavender bottom/right + 7x7 gray face; the ZOOM box additionally carries the nested-square 'little dude' glyph, the CLOSE box does not.

dialog_dboxproc_border.note: dBoxProc (modal dialog) fancy border is 7 px wide. Distinct from documentProc (standard document window) which uses the 1 px frame + 1 px groove.

drop_shadow_documentproc.note: documentProc (varCode=0) drop shadow is 1 px at offset (1,1). WDEF code: 'move.l OneOne,D4 / tst.w varCodeReg / beq.s @1'. Other variants: dBoxProc without title (varCode=1) uses 2x2 shadow; some variants use no shadow.

small_icon_in_title.note: Width of the small (SICN) icon in the title bar. Corresponds to minTitleHIcon EQU 21 (the title bar is 1 px taller when this icon is present).

pinstripe_period.fetch_status: BRIEF-SOURCED from community CSS recreation + v0 frame measurement (gui-ground-truth.md Sec 3.3); consistent with WDEF wTitleBarLight/wTitleBarDark shade index pairs (FIRST-HAND VERIFIED -- WDEF defines wTitleBarLight EQU 7 and wTitleBarDark EQU 8 as the alternating shade indices)
pinstripe_period.note: 1 dark + 1 light alternating scanline pattern; total period = 2 px. The WDEF source confirms the light/dark pairing via wTitleBarLight EQU 7 / wTitleBarDark EQU 8 (first-hand verified). The exact RGB values for those shade indices (stored in the wctb resource ID -4096) are golden-resolves -- they cannot be derived from WDEF alone and need a real System 7 screendump with wctb resource extraction via ResEdit.

titlebar_shade_indices.fetch_status: cited; values match documented System-7 constants
titlebar_shade_indices.note: WDEF constants wTitleBarLight EQU 7 and wTitleBarDark EQU 8 are the indices into the wctb (window color table) resource ID -4096 for the alternating pinstripe shades. The wctb id=0 (full 7.0.1 distribution + 7.5.3, byte-identical) carries wTitleBarLight (idx 7) = #FFFFFF and wTitleBarDark (idx 8) = #000000, confirming the stripe gray is a DITHER of those two anchors -- not a palette entry. See native.pinstripe_light_rgb / native.pinstripe_dark_rgb for the rendered values. Ref: ../system7-decomp/specs/chrome/pinstripe.md; re/mint-results-006.md [verified: wctb_0_System_colorHD.bin == wctb_0_System_753.bin]

pinstripe_light_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png
pinstripe_light_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the LIGHT (wTitleBarLight / wHiliteShade8 foreground) stripe row in an ACTIVE title bar. NOT pure white: the 8-bpp dither of the WDEF HilitePattern ($FF00 x4) over back-color wTitleBarShade1 produces this softened gray pair. Ref: pinstripe.md Sec 'Rendered colors'; WDEF @ 2206, 692, 861.

pinstripe_dark_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png
pinstripe_dark_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the DARK (wTitleBarDark / back-color wTitleBarShade1) stripe row. NOT pure black: the dither renders as solid #969696 horizontal rows (confirmed x=373..518 continuous at y=168). Ref: pinstripe.md Sec 'Rendered colors'.

pinstripe_bevel_top_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png; tinge SCHEME (#CCCCFF/#333366 anchors) 7.0-origin [verified: WDEF_0.bin DISASM @0xE0E/0xE1A/0xE20 + cctb_0; re/wdef-shade-table-disasm.md]
pinstripe_bevel_top_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the top bevel HIGHLIGHT row (wLTinge0, WDEF @ 711-744), drawn 1px inside the black outer frame. The 1px lavender row at the very top of the pinstripe interior. Ref: pinstripe.md Sec 'Rendered colors'; WDEF wLTinge0 (shade index 34).

pinstripe_bevel_bottom_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png; tinge SCHEME 7.0-origin [re/wdef-shade-table-disasm.md]
pinstripe_bevel_bottom_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the bottom bevel SHADOW row (wLTinge4, WDEF @ 711-744), drawn 1px inside the black outer frame at the base of the title bar interior. Ref: pinstripe.md Sec 'Rendered colors'; WDEF wLTinge4.

close_zoom_box_dark_outline_rgb.corpus_golden: goldens/captures/s7_doc_window.png @ x=361,y=170 (IDLE close box left edge)
close_zoom_box_dark_outline_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png; tinge SCHEME 7.0-origin [re/wdef-shade-table-disasm.md]
close_zoom_box_dark_outline_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the dark blue-violet outline of the close (goAway) and zoom boxes in IDLE state. NOT pure black. The outer top/left and inner right(x=370)/bottom(y=177) ring of the 3-D beveled gadget. Applies to both close and zoom boxes. Ref: close-zoom-box.md Sec 'IDLE state'.

close_zoom_box_bevel_highlight_rgb.corpus_golden: goldens/captures/s7_doc_window.png @ x=362,y=169 (UL bevel of IDLE close box)
close_zoom_box_bevel_highlight_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png; tinge SCHEME 7.0-origin [re/wdef-shade-table-disasm.md]
close_zoom_box_bevel_highlight_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the inner lavender bevel HIGHLIGHT of the close/zoom box gadget in IDLE state. Matches the title-bar bevel top highlight (same wLTinge0 family). Applies to both close and zoom boxes. Ref: close-zoom-box.md Sec 'IDLE state'.

close_zoom_box_face_rgb.corpus_golden: goldens/captures/s7_doc_window.png @ x=366,y=172 (7x7 gray face of IDLE close box)
close_zoom_box_face_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md; also confirmed by grow-box interior scan (s7_growbox.png)
close_zoom_box_face_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the recessed 7x7 interior face of the close/zoom box in IDLE state. The wTitleBarShade4 blend. Ref: close-zoom-box.md Sec 'IDLE state'.

close_zoom_box_pressed_interior_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png; tinge SCHEME 7.0-origin [re/wdef-shade-table-disasm.md]
close_zoom_box_pressed_interior_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the close/zoom box interior in PRESSED (mousedown) state. The hilit/pressed tone used by wGoAwayHilitedGadget / wZoomHilitedGadget. PRESSED also changes the outline from #545487 to solid #000000. Identical for close and zoom boxes. Ref: close-zoom-box.md Sec 'PRESSED state'; re/mint-results-004.md sec 4.

grow_box_ground_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png
grow_box_ground_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the DrawGrowIcon grow-box background field (wTitleBarShade1 = shade index 22). Same wTitleBarShade1 value as the pinstripe light row. The grow box ACTIVE ground: the WDEF erases to this shade before plotting the nested-box glyph. Ref: grow-box.md Sec 'Rendered colors'; re/mint-results-004.md sec 3.

grow_box_figure_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png; tinge SCHEME 7.0-origin [re/wdef-shade-table-disasm.md]
grow_box_figure_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the nested-box figure outlines (wFrameColor / wDTingeF) in the active DrawGrowIcon gadget. Same dark blue-violet as the close/zoom box outline (#545487). Ref: grow-box.md Sec 'Rendered colors'; re/mint-results-004.md sec 3.

grow_box_highlight_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png; tinge SCHEME 7.0-origin [re/wdef-shade-table-disasm.md]
grow_box_highlight_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the wLTinge0 light highlight edges in the DrawGrowIcon gadget (the lower/right light edges of the outer nested box, per the two-box ascii art in WDEF @ 1642-1648). Ref: grow-box.md Sec 'Rendered colors'; re/mint-results-004.md sec 3.

grow_box_inner_fill_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md
grow_box_inner_fill_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the inner nested-box interior fill (wTitleBarShade4 / wDTingeF center, WDEF @ 1617-1627). Same value as the close/zoom box face (#C0C0C0). A secondary lighter shade #CDCDCD (205,205,205) also appears (9 px) in the grow cell as wTitleBarShade4 family variation; see grow_box_secondary_rgb. Ref: grow-box.md Sec 'Rendered colors'.

grow_box_secondary_rgb.era_stability: verified: golden goldens/captures/s7_growbox.png; era 7.5.3; cite re/mint-results-004.md sec 3
grow_box_secondary_rgb.note: Secondary interior shading within the grow box (wTitleBarShade4 family variation, 9 px). Appears in addition to the primary #C0C0C0 fill; exact WDEF shade assignment is a minor variant of wTitleBarShade4. Lower confidence than primary values; era-stable to 7.0.1 assumed via the wTitleBarShade4 family but not independently re/mint-005 confirmed.

scrollbar_active_thumb_light_rgb.corpus_golden: goldens/captures/s7_scrollbar_active.png; 50/50 dither confirmed 24/24 px sample x=402..407,y=84..92 (mint-004 2026-06-20)
scrollbar_active_thumb_light_rgb.era_stability: verified era-stable System 7.0.1 (IIci ROM, native boot) -- ZERO delta vs 7.5.3 goldens; re/mint-results-005.md + goldens/captures/s7_701_doc_window_8bpp.png; tinge SCHEME 7.0-origin [re/wdef-shade-table-disasm.md]
scrollbar_active_thumb_light_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the LIGHT row of the active scrollbar thumb face dither. The thumb is a 50/50 alternating dither of this value and scrollbar_active_thumb_dark_rgb (#8787B3). The light value (#DADAFF) is era-stable to 7.0.1 [verified: re/mint-results-005.md]. Ref: scrollbar.md Sec 'Active scroll bar'; re/mint-results-004.md sec 2.

scrollbar_active_thumb_dark_rgb.era_stability_note: LOW CONFIDENCE for 7.0/7.1: #8787B3 thumb-face dither partner not independently measured in re/mint-results-005.md (only #DADAFF was confirmed era-stable in that mint). The 7.5.3 value is [verified: golden s7_scrollbar_active.png; re/mint-results-004.md sec 2]; era-stability to 7.0/7.1 is [golden-resolves: needs bootable 7.0.1/7.1 image with active overflowing window].
scrollbar_active_thumb_dark_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the DARK row of the active scrollbar thumb face dither. Part of the 50/50 lavender dither (#DADAFF / #8787B3). Era-stability to 7.0/7.1 is [golden-resolves]. Ref: scrollbar.md Sec 'Active scroll bar'; re/mint-results-004.md sec 2.

scrollbar_active_track_light_rgb.era_stability: verified: golden goldens/captures/s7_scrollbar_active.png; era 7.5.3; cite re/mint-results-004.md sec 2
scrollbar_active_track_light_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the LIGHT element (2/3) of the active scrollbar track gutter dither (#E7E7E7 / #969696, 2:1 ratio). Also used as the enabled arrow-box face fill. Ref: scrollbar.md Sec 'Active scroll bar'; re/mint-results-004.md sec 2.

scrollbar_active_track_dark_rgb.era_stability: verified: golden goldens/captures/s7_scrollbar_active.png; era 7.5.3; cite re/mint-results-004.md sec 2
scrollbar_active_track_dark_rgb.note: Rendered RGB of the DARK element (1/3) of the active scrollbar track gutter dither. Same shade as the pinstripe dark row (#969696) -- they share the wTitleBarDark family. Ref: scrollbar.md Sec 'Active scroll bar'; re/mint-results-004.md sec 2.

win31_sm_cycaption.corpus_golden: goldens/screenshots/clean/w31_client.png y=52-69 x=300 (ProgMan) + y=94-111 x=300 (MDI child) -- two independent measurements agree
win31_sm_cycaption.note: Windows 3.1 SM_CYCAPTION (caption bar interior height). 18 rows of VGA blue (#000080); 1px black border above (SM_CYBORDER) and below are NOT counted. Replaces the prior [golden-resolves] status: value was blocked pending DOSBox-X measurement; now resolved by the ../win31-decomp corpus golden (M-A row 1 + row 5). Ref: ../win31-decomp/specs/chrome/system-metrics.md Sec 1.

win31_sm_cxvscroll.corpus_golden: goldens/screenshots/clean/w31_listbox_scrollbar.png -- left border x=586, face x=587-600 (14 px); 16-color VGA artifacts reduce confidence
win31_sm_cxvscroll.refuted_claim: The widely cited '17 px' web claim is REFUTED -- that value is a Win 95/NT figure NOT confirmed in any Win 3.1 golden. Ref: system-metrics.md Sec 1 + Sec 5 cross-version-guardrails.
win31_sm_cxvscroll.note: Windows 3.1 SM_CXVSCROLL (vertical scrollbar width). Best measurement from current 16-color goldens is ~15 px; low confidence due to VGA-16 color artifacts making the left scrollbar border hard to isolate. Definitive value requires 256-color S3 re-mint. Replacing the prior [golden-resolves] block: the 17px web claim is now actively REFUTED; the value 15 is the best available estimate. Ref: ../win31-decomp/specs/chrome/system-metrics.md Sec 3 (mint recipe) + Sec 1.

_description: Metrics that CANNOT be sourced from WDEF/IM or the sister corpora alone
These are TODOs -- do NOT invent pixel values.
Ref: ADR-0004 D-8 / FO-2 / AM-3; gui-ground-truth.md Sec 3.3, Sec 8.
RESOLVED in schema_version 3 (P1-4 uplift, beads initech-dh5k.4):

scrollbar_active_thumb_dark_7x_era.what: Era-stability confirmation of #8787B3 (scrollbar active thumb dark dither partner) for System 7.0/7.1 specifically.
scrollbar_active_thumb_dark_7x_era.why_blocked: The 7.5.3 value #8787B3 is verified from s7_scrollbar_active.png (mint-004). re/mint-results-005.md confirmed #DADAFF (the light partner) is era-stable to 7.0.1 but did NOT independently measure #8787B3 in the 7.0.1 active-thumb boot. The dark partner is therefore [golden-resolves] only for the 7.0/7.1 era claim.
scrollbar_active_thumb_dark_7x_era.acquisition_path: Boot Basilisk II with a 7.0.1 or 7.1 System file; open a window whose content overflows the view (so an active scroll thumb appears); capture active scroll bar golden; measure thumb face dither dark partner.
scrollbar_active_thumb_dark_7x_era.current_best: #8787B3 from 7.5.3 golden (native.scrollbar_active_thumb_dark_rgb); use this value pending era-confirmation.

grow_box_inactive_blank.what: Confirmation that the grow box is completely blank (WDEF erases to content color) on a non-front (inactive) growable window.
grow_box_inactive_blank.why_blocked: WDEF code documents EraseRect at 1487-1492 (inactive path), but no two-growable-window golden exists to visually confirm the back window's lower-right corner is blank vs dimmed.
grow_box_inactive_blank.acquisition_path: Mint a golden with two stacked growable windows; measure the back window's grow corner.
grow_box_inactive_blank.inferred: WDEF @ 1487-1492 documents the inactive erase path; functional behavior is documented, pixel appearance not yet confirmed.

win31_sm_cxvscroll_definitive.what: Definitive pixel count for SM_CXVSCROLL at 256-color depth (the 16-color golden gives ~15 px with low confidence due to color artifacts).
win31_sm_cxvscroll_definitive.why_blocked: Current golden (w31_listbox_scrollbar.png) is 16-color VGA; bright-blue VGA-16 listbox selection merges with scrollbar face pixels, making the left border hard to isolate. Definitive value requires 256-color S3/SVGA re-mint.
win31_sm_cxvscroll_definitive.acquisition_path: Install the S3/SVGA-256 Win 3.1 display driver via ProgMan > Windows Setup > Change System Settings > Display. Reboot DOSBox-X at 640x480x256. Re-open the printer list dialog (listbox with vertical scrollbar). Capture a clean crop. Measure scrollbar width. See ../win31-decomp/specs/chrome/system-metrics.md Sec 3.
win31_sm_cxvscroll_definitive.current_best: ~15 px from 16-color golden (native.win31_sm_cxvscroll); 17 px web claim ACTIVELY REFUTED.
